summary: Re-review: F1 RESOLVED — bert changed the combine to max (De Morgan dual of the original OR guards); overdue only when past BOTH, R50.0 period-boundary extension restored; null-handling + call-site comparison verified consistent. 0 unresolved BLOCK.

steps.scooter_consult: n/a (R50.0 + 999.671 read as LOCKED from SPEC governing rules + memory juggler-never-missing-invariant; F1 contradicts R50.0)

findings.claim: RESOLVED (was BLOCK): computeEffectiveDeadline now returns max(periodBoundary, windowClose), the De Morgan dual of the original two-guard OR semantics — overdue only when past BOTH deadlines; R50.0 period-boundary extension restored.
findings.evidence: Helper :240 `return periodBoundary > windowClose ? periodBoundary : windowClose` (later of two non-null); :238-239 ignore nulls (one null->the other, both null->null). Call-site :1943 `if (!effectiveDeadline || today < effectiveDeadline) return` => overdue iff today >= max(periodEnd, windowClose) = past BOTH, consistent with max. R50.0: periodEnd > windowClose => effectiveDeadline = periodEnd, flexible-TPC instance stays live until cycle boundary. grep confirms no residual min/'earlier' semantics; doc :220-228 + call-site comment :1932-1937 corrected to max/De Morgan.
findings.fix: None — resolved by bert. SPEC AC-840-4 (which prescribed min) still needs Kermit/Scooter reconciliation to match the shipped max; tracked via F1 refer to kermit.

findings.claim: time_flex != null admits negative flex, which would make window-close precede the slot and fire overdue prematurely.
findings.evidence: taskValidation.js:152 rejects tfVal<0 (range 0..480) at the API boundary, so negatives cannot persist through the supported path; defense-in-depth note only.
findings.fix: No change required; relies on the documented validation invariant.

findings.claim: checkPlacementDisjointness silently under-reports when an entry has missing/NaN dur or start (NaN > next.start === false).
findings.evidence: aEnd = prev.start + prev.dur; if prev.dur is undefined aEnd is NaN and the comparison is false, so a malformed placement produces no warn. Off-by-one is correct (> not >=, touching allowed); single-entry/empty/null/unsorted all handled.
findings.fix: Optional: coerce/guard prev.dur and start to numbers and warn on non-numeric. Acceptable for a non-blocking diagnostic.

findings.claim: The F1 regression scenario (periodEnd later than windowClose, with windowClose < today < periodEnd) has no covering test, which is why the min/max inversion passed green.
findings.evidence: BERT-LOG: 'no existing test covers the flexible-TPC-with-timeFlex scenario; behavior for day-locked instances unchanged.'
findings.fix: REFER->telly: add a regression test that goes RED on the current min() and asserts the instance stays live until periodEnd.

findings.claim: computeWindowCloseUtc treats preferred_time_mins as UTC-minutes-since-midnight; a local-tz stored value would skew the window-close in the cal-history cron consumer.
findings.evidence: Function anchors to saDate UTC midnight + preferredMins; comment asserts UTC-pure. The in-scheduler auto-mark consumer was removed in Leg D, leaving only the missedHelpers cron.

refers.why: SPEC AC-840-4 prescribes min(), which contradicts LOCKED R50.0; reconcile before bert re-fixes F1.
Missing flexible-TPC period-boundary regression test that would catch the F1 min/max inversion.
